_comment: abbreviation/acronym -> full expansion, keyed case-sensitively where casing is part of the identity of an acronym (e.g. URA), and case-insensitively for ordinary titles (see abbreviations.py). Same vetting policy as spelling_dictionary.json: only add entries that are confirmed, stable, public facts (organization names) or well-established cross-language abbreviation conventions -- do not guess informal SMS-speak shorthand without native-speaker sign-off. NOTE: load_abbreviations() in abbreviations.py only reads the 'organizations', 'titles', and 'cross_language' keys -- government agencies, ministries, and international bodies are all filed under 'organizations' since they share the same case-sensitive matching behavior.
_comment_translation_risk: Everything in 'organizations' is a verified official English full name -- no translation risk. Entries in 'titles' and 'cross_language' involve an actual Luganda translation and should get a native-speaker check before production use, especially: Rev, Amb, Fr, Sr, and every 'cross_language' entry beyond the original etc/e.g./i.e.

organizations.PPDA: Public Procurement and Disposal of Public Assets Authority
